question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan] Can an unprivileged market creator or taker under a deployed Midnight instance with no token balance restrictions call flashLoan(tokens, assets, callback, data) with tokens.length set to an arbitrarily large value (e.g., 10_000 entries), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer and SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom to be invoked once per token in two separate unbounded for-loops (lines 742-751 of Midnight.sol), violating the invariant that protocol entry-points must not expose unbounded O(n) external-call loops to callers, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where any caller composing flashLoan inside a multicall or callback can be forced into OOG by choosing a large tokens array? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 10_000 with a no-op callback and assert gas consumed grows linearly; confirm OOG at block gas limit.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment call flashLoan with the same token address repeated N times in the tokens array, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to send N*assets[i] of that token to the callback and SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom to pull N*assets[i] back, violating the invariant that a single flashLoan call must not transfer more than the requested amount of any single token, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where duplicate token entries bypass per-token balance checks and could drain protocol reserves if the callback only repays one occurrence? Proof idea: unit test flashLoan([token, token], [amount, amount], callback, data) and assert Midnight's token balance is unchanged after the call.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall] Can an unprivileged taker under a Midnight deployment call multicall(calls) with calls.length set to an arbitrarily large value where each call encodes a take or repay that triggers SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom, causing the multicall for-loop (Midnight.sol line 212) to execute O(N) delegatecalls each containing at least one external ERC20 call, violating the invariant that multicall must not expose unbounded gas consumption to callers, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a victim composing a multicall with many operations is forced into OOG by an attacker who front-runs with a state change that makes each inner call more expensive? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to 1_000 with identical take calls and assert gas grows linearly.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate / isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market with MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER=16 collaterals activate all 16 collateral slots via supplyCollateral, causing the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop in liquidate (Midnight.sol line 607) to call IOracle.price() 16 times before reaching SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer and SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where the gas cost of 16 oracle calls plus two SafeTransferLib calls exceeds the liquidation incentive, making liquidation economically unviable and leaving bad debt unrealized? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles with high SLOAD cost, activate all 16 collaterals, measure liquidate gas, compare to LIF-derived profit.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy (called from take)] Can an unprivileged taker under a market where the seller has all 16 collateral slots activated call take against that seller, causing isHealthy (Midnight.sol line 476) to execute the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop 16 times with 16 IOracle.price() external calls after the two SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom calls, violating the invariant that take must complete within a predictable gas budget, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a borrower with 16 activated collaterals makes every take against them prohibitively expensive, effectively freezing the market for that seller? Proof idea: fuzz collateralBitmap popcount from 1 to 16 and measure take gas; assert gas at popcount=16 exceeds a reasonable block fraction.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator under a Midnight deployment call touchMarket(market) with market.collateralParams.length = MAX_COLLATERALS = 128, causing the for-loop (Midnight.sol line 762) to iterate 128 times checking collateral addresses, LLTV values, and maxLif values, and then any subsequent call to take, repay, or liquidate that passes the same 128-collateral Market struct in calldata to incur O(128) ABI decoding cost plus the loop cost, violating the invariant that market creation and subsequent operations must not impose unbounded gas costs on users, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where all operations on a 128-collateral market are significantly more expensive than on a 1-collateral market? Proof idea: create markets with collateralParams.length in [1, 16, 64, 128] and measure take gas; assert gas scales with collateral count.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer / safeTransferFrom] Can an unprivileged attacker under a market using a malicious ERC20 token that returns a very large bytes payload (e.g., 100KB) from transfer() cause SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer (line 15: bytes memory returndata = token.call(...)) to copy the entire returndata into memory, triggering quadratic memory expansion gas costs, violating the invariant that SafeTransferLib must not allow a token to cause OOG in any protocol function, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where withdraw, withdrawCollateral, liquidate, or claimSettlementFee becomes permanently uncallable due to OOG from the returndata bomb? Proof idea: deploy a token whose transfer() returns abi.encode(bytes(100_000)), call withdraw, and assert OOG or measure memory expansion cost.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom] Can an unprivileged attacker under a market using a malicious ERC20 token that returns a very large bytes payload from transferFrom() cause SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom (line 27: bytes memory returndata = token.call(...)) to copy the entire returndata into memory, triggering quadratic memory expansion gas costs, violating the invariant that SafeTransferLib must not allow a token to cause OOG in any protocol function that pulls tokens, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where take, repay, supplyCollateral, or liquidate becomes permanently uncallable due to OOG from the returndata bomb in safeTransferFrom? Proof idea: deploy a token whose transferFrom() returns abi.encode(bytes(100_000)), call repay, and assert OOG or measure memory expansion cost.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + flashLoan] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall(calls) where each call encodes a flashLoan with a large tokens array, causing nested O(N*M) SafeTransferLib calls (N multicall iterations * M flashLoan token iterations), violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify gas consumption beyond the sum of individual call costs, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a single multicall transaction with N=100 calls each containing M=100 tokens triggers 20_000 external ERC20 calls, exhausting the block gas limit? Proof idea: fuzz N and M independently, measure total gas, assert OOG threshold is reachable with small N*M product.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market with 128 collateral params create a position with 16 activated collaterals where each collateral's oracle is a contract that performs expensive computation (e.g., 10 SLOADs) in price(), causing the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop in liquidate to consume 16 * (oracle_cost + mulDivDown overhead) gas before reaching SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) at line 696, violating the invariant that liquidation must remain economically viable for liquidators, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where the borrower's position becomes permanently unliquidatable due to gas cost exceeding block gas limit or liquidation profit? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles each consuming 50_000 gas, activate all 16, measure liquidate gas, assert it exceeds 15M gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy (called from withdrawCollateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market with 16 activated collaterals call withdrawCollateral, causing isHealthy (Midnight.sol line 568) to execute the while(_collateralBitmap != 0) loop 16 times with 16 IOracle.price() external calls, and then SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) at line 572, violating the invariant that withdrawCollateral must complete within a predictable gas budget, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a borrower with 16 activated collaterals makes withdrawCollateral prohibitively expensive for themselves or for authorized operators? Proof idea: fuzz collateralBitmap popcount from 1 to 16 and measure withdrawCollateral gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment call flashLoan(tokens, assets, callback, data) where assets[i] = 0 for all i and tokens.length is large, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to be called with value=0 for each token (still performing extcodesize check + external call), violating the invariant that zero-value transfers must not impose unbounded gas costs, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where an attacker can grief any flashLoan caller by front-running with a state change that forces zero-value transfers in a large loop? Proof idea: call flashLoan with tokens.length=1000 and assets=[0]*1000, measure gas, assert it grows linearly with tokens.length.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker under a market with 128 collateral params compose a multicall with N supplyCollateral calls each for a different collateral index, causing N SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom calls and N collateralBitmap.setBit operations, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow a single transaction to activate more than MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER=16 collaterals (TooManyActivatedCollaterals check), causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the bitmap check in supplyCollateral (line 538-540) is bypassed if the multicall processes collateral activations in a sequence that passes the per-call check but collectively exceeds the limit? Proof idea: multicall 17 supplyCollateral calls for 17 distinct collateral indices and assert TooManyActivatedCollaterals is thrown on the 17th.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market with multiple borrowers each having 16 activated collaterals compose a multicall with N liquidate calls, causing N * (16 oracle calls + safeTransfer + safeTransferFrom) gas consumption, violating the invariant that multicall must not amplify per-call gas costs beyond block limits, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a liquidator attempting to liquidate N positions in one transaction is forced into OOG by the O(N*16) oracle call overhead? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 50 with 16-collateral borrowers, measure multicall gas, assert OOG threshold.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in liquidate, line 696)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market where the collateral token is a contract that consumes high gas in transfer() (e.g., an ERC777 with expensive hooks) cause SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) in liquidate to consume excessive gas, violating the invariant that liquidation must remain economically viable, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure where the collateral token's transfer cost makes liquidation unprofitable or causes OOG, leaving the borrower's unhealthy position permanently unliquidatable? Proof idea: deploy a collateral token whose transfer() consumes 200_000 gas, create an unhealthy position, call liquidate, and assert gas cost exceeds liquidation incentive.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in liquidate, line 696) + safeTransferFrom (line 717)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market observe that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) is called BEFORE the liquidate callback and BEFORE SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits), causing a state where collateral has been sent to the receiver but the loan token has not yet been received, violating the invariant that collateral seizure and debt repayment must be atomic, causing scoped impact: direct loss where a callback that reverts after receiving collateral but before the safeTransferFrom causes the liquidation to revert with collateral already transferred? Proof idea: deploy a callback that reverts in onLiquidate after receiving collateral, call liquidate, assert the full transaction reverts and no collateral is lost.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket + take] Can an unprivileged market creator under a Midnight deployment create a market with market.collateralParams.length = 128 and then call take with an Offer containing that market, causing the ABI decoding of the 128-element CollateralParams[] array from calldata to consume O(128) gas on every take call, violating the invariant that market creation parameters must not impose unbounded per-call overhead on subsequent operations, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where all takers in a 128-collateral market pay significantly higher gas than in a 1-collateral market, making the market economically unviable? Proof idea: create markets with 1, 16, 64, 128 collaterals, call take on each, measure gas difference attributable to calldata decoding.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + withdraw] Can an unprivileged lender under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall with N withdraw calls each for 1 unit, causing N SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, 1) calls, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow a single transaction to drain withdrawable reserves through N micro-withdrawals that each pass the individual credit check but collectively exceed the lender's credit balance, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the credit underflow check in withdraw (line 493: _position.credit -= UtilsLib.toUint128(units)) is hit on the Nth call but the first N-1 calls have already transferred tokens? Proof idea: multicall N withdraw(1) calls where N > credit balance and assert the transaction reverts on the Nth call with no partial state.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (NoCode check)] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment observe that SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer and SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom both perform require(token.code.length > 0, NoCode()) (lines 13 and 25), and exploit this by creating a market with a loan token that is an EOA address (code.length == 0), causing every take, withdraw, repay, and liquidate to revert with NoCode(), violating the invariant that market creation must validate that loan and collateral tokens are contracts, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where all funds deposited into a market with a non-contract token are permanently locked? Proof idea: create a market with loanToken = address(0x1234) (EOA), supply collateral, take an offer, assert NoCode() revert on the safeTransferFrom.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + take (128-collateral market)] Can an unprivileged taker under a 128-collateral market compose a multicall with N take calls each passing the full 128-element CollateralParams[] in the Offer.market struct, causing N * O(128) calldata decoding overhead plus N * isHealthy calls (each potentially looping 16 times over the seller's collateral bitmap), violating the invariant that multicall gas cost must be bounded by the sum of individual call costs without amplification from large struct decoding, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a taker composing N takes in a 128-collateral market is forced into OOG? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 20 with a 128-collateral market and 16-collateral seller, measure multicall gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in take, lines 455-456)] Can an unprivileged taker under a market where the loan token is a fee-on-transfer ERC20 call take, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) to transfer less than buyerAssets - sellerAssets to Midnight due to the fee, while the accounting records the full buyerAssets - sellerAssets as claimableSettlementFee, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must not exceed actual token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where the fee claimer cannot claim the full recorded settlement fee because the actual balance is less? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer token, execute N takes, assert claimableSettlementFee[token] > Midnight.balanceOf(token).

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in repay, line 520)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market where the loan token is a fee-on-transfer ERC20 call repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), ''), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, msg.sender, address(this), units) to transfer less than units to Midnight, while position[id][onBehalf].debt is reduced by the full units and marketState[id].withdrawable is increased by the full units, violating the invariant that withdrawable must not exceed actual token balance, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where lenders can withdraw more than the actual token balance? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer token, repay N units, assert withdrawable > Midnight.balanceOf(loanToken).

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in supplyCollateral, line 545)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market where the collateral token is a fee-on-transfer ERC20 call supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets) to transfer less than assets to Midnight, while position[id][onBehalf].collateral[collateralIndex] is increased by the full assets, violating the invariant that recorded collateral must not exceed actual token balance, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation where the borrower's collateral is overvalued relative to actual holdings, allowing undercollateralized borrowing? Proof idea: deploy a 1% fee-on-transfer collateral token, supply N assets, assert position.collateral > Midnight.balanceOf(collateralToken).

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in withdraw, line 499)] Can an unprivileged lender under a market where the loan token is a rebasing ERC20 (balance increases over time) call withdraw(market, units, onBehalf, receiver), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, units) to transfer exactly units while the actual balance has grown, violating the invariant that lenders must be able to claim their full share of rebased tokens, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the rebased portion of the loan token balance is permanently locked in Midnight with no accounting entry to claim it? Proof idea: deploy a rebasing token, lend units, rebase up by 10%, withdraw units, assert the 10% rebase is stuck in Midnight.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + claimSettlementFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall with N claimSettlementFee calls each for amount=1, causing N SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(token, receiver, 1) calls, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] must not go below zero (underflow protection via Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic), causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the fee claimer's multicall reverts on the Nth call if N > claimableSettlementFee[token], but the first N-1 calls have already transferred tokens? Proof idea: multicall N claimSettlementFee(token, 1, receiver) calls where N > claimableSettlementFee[token] and assert the full transaction reverts atomically.
